meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't debug code
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome debugging hurdles. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster.
content.answer_block: Stop wrestling with cryptic stack traces. By implementing a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you abstract away the boilerplate that causes 90% of bugs. Pair this with AI-driven debugging tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to instantly diagnose logic errors, ensuring your client projects remain stable, scalable, and delivered on time.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, the client deadline is six hours away, and your terminal is bleeding red error messages you don't recognize. You've spent three hours chasing a ghost in your authentication flow, and the frustration is palpable. This is the 'debugging trap' that kills freelance careers before they even gain momentum.

But what if you stopped building from scratch and started building on a foundation of certainty? A high-quality SaaS starter kit isn't just a convenience; it's a structural insurance policy. By leveraging automated architecture and AI-assisted diagnostics, you can transform your workflow from reactive fire-fighting to proactive, high-velocity shipping.

steps.title: Initialize with a Production-Grade Starter
steps.description: Select a boilerplate that includes pre-configured authentication, database ORM, and error boundaries to prevent common runtime failures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a SaaS project starter with Next.js, TypeScript, and Supabase, ensuring all error logging middleware is pre-configured.

steps.title: Implement AI-Assisted Error Monitoring
steps.description: Integrate logging hooks that automatically pipe stack traces to your AI agent for immediate triage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an error boundary component that catches client-side exceptions and formats them for AI analysis.

steps.title: Isolate Logic with Modular Services
steps.description: Decouple your business logic from the UI to make debugging specific functions significantly easier.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current auth service into a standalone, testable module that handles its own edge cases.

steps.title: Automated Debugging with Claude Code
steps.description: When a bug occurs, feed the terminal output directly to Claude Code to get a root cause analysis instead of manual trial and error.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the recent build failure logs in the terminal and suggest a fix for the circular dependency issue.

steps.title: Regression Testing Suite
steps.description: Automate tests for your core features so that new code doesn't break the foundation you just built.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of Playwright tests for the critical path of the user onboarding flow.

pro_tips: Always wrap your external API calls in a decorator that logs latency and error headers; this cuts debugging time by 70%.
Use 'dry-run' modes in your CI/CD pipeline to catch environment variable mismatches before they hit production.
Treat your error logs as data; build a simple dashboard to see which features fail most often for end-users.
Configure your IDE to show types on hover—many 'bugs' are just type mismatches that TypeScript can catch before runtime.
Never manually fix a bug twice; if it happens again, write a specific unit test to ensure it never returns.
content.next_steps: Once your project is stable, focus on setting up automated deployment pipelines to ensure your client projects remain bug-free post-launch.
content.conversion_block: Stop burning hours on manual fixes and start shipping like a pro. With the right starter kit, you can reclaim your time and scale your freelance business effortlessly. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
